message.content:
You are implementing Task 2 of the ZH-Copilot replay scraper. Repo: /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot. Venv: .venv (activate with `source .venv/bin/activate`). Python 3.13. Playwright and BeautifulSoup4 are installed. **Context:** We're building a scraper for GameReplays.org Zero Hour replays. Task 2 implements the HTML parser for listing pages — pure functions, no network calls, fully testable. **CONFIRMED HTML STRUCTURE** (crawled live April 2026): Each replay is a bare `
  • ` with NO CSS classes. Fields are positional: ```html
  • 0 3 1 saptac HARD^^ Usa mirror vs HARD By: saptac - Added 26 Mar, 2026 - v1.04
    • ``` **Step 1: Save listing page HTML fixture** Run this Python to fetch and save the fixture: ```bash cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot && source .venv/bin/activate && python - <<'PYEOF' from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright with sync_playwright() as p: browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=True) page = browser.new_page() page.goto("https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php?game=2&show=index&tab_new=upcoming&sort_by=date_added&st=0", wait_until="networkidle", timeout=30000) html = page.content() browser.close() with open("tests/data/fixtures/listing_page.html", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: f.write(html) print(f"Saved {len(html)} chars") PYEOF ``` **Step 2: Write failing tests** Create `tests/data/test_gamereplays.py`: ```python # tests/data/test_gamereplays.py """Tests for GameReplays.org HTML parsers.""" from pathlib import Path import pytest from zh_copilot.data.gamereplays import parse_listing_page, listing_page_url FIXTURES = Path(__file__).parent / "fixtures" @pytest.fixture def listing_html(): return (FIXTURES / "listing_page.html").read_text(encoding="utf-8") def test_parse_listing_returns_list(listing_html): results = parse_listing_page(listing_html) assert isinstance(results, list) assert len(results) > 0 def test_parse_listing_entry_has_required_fields(listing_html): results = parse_listing_page(listing_html) entry = results[0] for field in ["id", "title", "uploader", "upload_date", "factions", "players", "download_count", "wubs", "game_version"]: assert field in entry, f"Missing field: {field}" def test_parse_listing_id_is_int(listing_html): results = parse_listing_page(listing_html) assert isinstance(results[0]["id"], int) assert results[0]["id"] > 0 def test_parse_listing_download_count_is_int(listing_html): results = parse_listing_page(listing_html) assert isinstance(results[0]["download_count"], int) def test_parse_listing_factions_is_list(listing_html): results = parse_listing_page(listing_html) assert isinstance(results[0]["factions"], list) def test_parse_listing_players_is_list(listing_html): results = parse_listing_page(listing_html) assert isinstance(results[0]["players"], list) def test_parse_listing_no_duplicate_ids(listing_html): results = parse_listing_page(listing_html) ids = [r["id"] for r in results] assert len(ids) == len(set(ids)) def test_parse_listing_returns_up_to_30(listing_html): results = parse_listing_page(listing_html) assert 1 <= len(results) <= 30 def test_listing_page_url_pagination(): url0 = listing_page_url(offset=0) url1 = listing_page_url(offset=30) assert "st=0" in url0 assert "st=30" in url1 assert "game=2" in url0 def test_listing_page_url_unauthenticated(): url = listing_page_url(offset=0, authenticated=False) assert "tab_new=upcoming" in url assert "search_submit" not in url def test_listing_page_url_authenticated(): url = listing_page_url(offset=0, authenticated=True) assert "search_submit" in url assert "tab_new" not in url ``` **Step 3: Run tests to see failure** ```bash cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest tests/data/test_gamereplays.py -v 2>&1 | head -20 ``` Expected: ImportError. **Step 4: Implement `src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py`** Create this file with the exact content below. The parser is written to match the confirmed bare-`
  • ` HTML structure: ```python # src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py """HTML parsers for GameReplays.org — pure functions, no network calls. Actual HTML structure (confirmed April 2026): Each replay is a bare
  • with no CSS classes. Fields are positional:
  • {wubs} 3 1 Player1 Player2 Title text By: uploader - Added DD Mon, YYYY - vX.XX
    • """ from __future__ import annotations import re from typing import Any from bs4 import BeautifulSoup, NavigableString _BASE = "https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php" _GAME = 2 # Zero Hour game ID _LISTING_URL_NOAUTH = ( f"{_BASE}?game={_GAME}&show=index&tab_new=upcoming&sort_by=date_added&st={{offset}}" ) _LISTING_URL_AUTH = ( f"{_BASE}?game={_GAME}&show=search_submit" f"&search=&matchup_type=all&army_1=all&army_2=all" f"&map=all&version=all&event=all&search_submit=clicked&st={{offset}}" ) def listing_page_url(offset: int = 0, authenticated: bool = False) -> str: """Return the listing page URL at the given offset. Args: offset: Pagination offset (increments of 30). authenticated: If True, uses the search endpoint (35,998 replays, requires session). If False (default), uses public endpoint (12,366 replays). """ template = _LISTING_URL_AUTH if authenticated else _LISTING_URL_NOAUTH return template.format(offset=offset) def detail_page_url(replay_id: int) -> str: """Return the detail page URL for a replay ID.""" return f"{_BASE}?s=0&game={_GAME}&show=details&id={replay_id}" def download_url(replay_id: int) -> str: """Return the download URL for a replay ID.""" return f"{_BASE}?s=0&game={_GAME}&show=download&&id={replay_id}" def parse_listing_page(html: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]: """Parse a listing page and return a list of replay metadata dicts. Parses the bare
  • structure (no CSS classes) from the live site. Returns list of dicts with: id, title, uploader, upload_date, factions, players, download_count, wubs, game_version. """ soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser") results = [] seen_ids: set[int] = set() download_pat = re.compile(r"show=download") member_pat = re.compile(r"show=member_replays&(?:amp;)?member_id=") player_pat = re.compile(r"search_player=") id_pat = re.compile(r"[?&]id=(\d+)") for li in soup.find_all("li"): anchors = li.find_all("a", href=True) # Must contain a download link to be a replay row download_a = next((a for a in anchors if download_pat.search(a.get("href", ""))), None) if download_a is None: continue # Extract replay ID from any link containing id= replay_id = None for a in anchors: m = id_pat.search(a.get("href", "")) if m: replay_id = int(m.group(1)) break if replay_id is None or replay_id in seen_ids: continue seen_ids.add(replay_id) # Wubs: first text node in
  • that is a bare integer wubs = 0 for child in li.children: if isinstance(child, NavigableString): text = child.strip() if text.isdigit(): wubs = int(text) break # Download count: text content of the download link download_count = _parse_int(download_a.get_text(strip=True)) # Factions: alt text of tags (e.g. "Vanilla USA", "China Nuke") imgs = li.find_all("img") factions = [img.get("alt", "").strip() for img in imgs if img.get("alt", "").strip()] # Players: tags with search_player= in href players = [ a.get_text(strip=True) for a in anchors if player_pat.search(a.get("href", "")) ] # Uploader: with member_replays in href uploader_a = next((a for a in anchors if member_pat.search(a.get("href", ""))), None) uploader = uploader_a.get_text(strip=True) if uploader_a else "" # Full text for date/version/title extraction full_text = li.get_text(" ", strip=True) title = _extract_title(full_text, players, uploader) upload_date = _extract_date(full_text) game_version = _extract_version(full_text) results.append({ "id": replay_id, "title": title, "uploader": uploader, "upload_date": upload_date, "factions": factions, "players": players, "download_count": download_count, "wubs": wubs, "game_version": game_version, }) return results def parse_detail_page(html: str, replay_id: int) -> dict[str, Any]: """Parse a replay detail page and return full metadata. Returns dict with: id, title, uploader, upload_date, factions, players, matchup_type, map_name, game_version, download_count, wubs, duration_seconds. """ soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser") full_text = soup.get_text(" ", strip=True) title_tag = soup.find("h1") or soup.find("h2") title = title_tag.get_text(strip=True) if title_tag else _extract_title(full_text, [], "") imgs = soup.find_all("img", alt=True) factions = [ img["alt"].strip() for img in imgs if img["alt"].strip() and len(img["alt"].strip()) < 30 and not img["alt"].strip().startswith(("/", "http")) ] player_pat = re.compile(r"search_player=") players = [a.get_text(strip=True) for a in soup.find_all("a", href=player_pat)] member_pat = re.compile(r"show=member_replays&(?:amp;)?member_id=") uploader_a = soup.find("a", href=member_pat) uploader = uploader_a.get_text(strip=True) if uploader_a else "" map_name = _extract_text_after(full_text, r"Map[:\s]+([^\n|]+?)(?:\s*[-|]|$)") matchup_type = _detect_matchup_type(full_text) duration_seconds = _extract_duration(full_text) upload_date = _extract_date(full_text) game_version = _extract_version(full_text) download_count = _parse_int(_extract_text_after(full_text, r"(\d[\d,]*)\s+[Dd]ownload")) wubs = _parse_int(_extract_text_after(full_text, r"(\d+)\s+[Ww]ub")) return { "id": replay_id, "title": title, "uploader": uploader, "upload_date": upload_date, "factions": factions[:2], "players": players[:2], "matchup_type": matchup_type, "map_name": (map_name or "").strip(), "game_version": game_version, "download_count": download_count, "wubs": wubs, "duration_seconds": duration_seconds, } # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Private helpers # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def _extract_title(full_text: str, players: list[str], uploader: str) -> str: text = full_text # Remove "By: uploader - Added ..." suffix text = re.sub(r"\s*By:.*$", "", text, flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL).strip() # Remove player names for p in players: if p: text = text.replace(p, "").strip() # Remove leading numbers (wubs, download count, comment count) text = re.sub(r"^\s*(\d+\s*)+", "", text).strip() return text def _extract_date(text: str) -> str: m = re.search(r"Added\s+(\d{1,2}\s+\w+,?\s+\d{4})", text, re.IGNORECASE) if m: return m.group(1) m = re.search(r"(\d{1,2}\s+\w{3,},?\s+\d{4})", text) return m.group(1) if m else "" def _extract_version(text: str) -> str: m = re.search(r"v(\d+\.\d+)", text) return m.group(1) if m else "" def _extract_text_after(text: str, pattern: str) -> str | None: m = re.search(pattern, text, re.IGNORECASE) if m: return m.group(1).strip() if m.lastindex else m.group(0).strip() return None def _detect_matchup_type(text: str) -> str: for matchup in ["4v4", "3v3", "2v2", "1v1", "FFA"]: if matchup.lower() in text.lower(): return matchup return "1v1" def _extract_duration(text: str) -> int | None: m = re.search(r"[Dd]uration[:\s]+(\d+):(\d+)", text) if m: return int(m.group(1)) * 60 + int(m.group(2)) m = re.search(r"(\d+)\s*min(?:ute)?s?\s+(\d+)\s*sec", text, re.IGNORECASE) if m: return int(m.group(1)) * 60 + int(m.group(2)) m = re.search(r"(\d+)\s*min(?:ute)?s?", text, re.IGNORECASE) if m: return int(m.group(1)) * 60 return None def _parse_int(s: str | None) -> int: if not s: return 0 m = re.search(r"\d[\d,]*", str(s)) return int(m.group(0).replace(",", "")) if m else 0 ``` **Step 5: Install beautifulsoup4** ```bash cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot && source .venv/bin/activate && pip install beautifulsoup4 ``` **Step 6: Run tests** ```bash cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest tests/data/test_gamereplays.py -v ``` All tests must pass. If `parse_listing_page` returns 0 results from the real HTML, the `
  • ` structure is different from what was confirmed — inspect the saved fixture HTML and adjust the parser accordingly. The key signals to look for: - Links with `show=download` in href → replay rows - Links with `id=NNN` in href → replay ID - `` → faction names - Links with `search_player=` → player names - Links with `member_replays&member_id=` → uploader **Step 7: Commit** ```bash cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot git add src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py tests/data/test_gamereplays.py tests/data/fixtures/listing_page.html git commit -m "feat(data): GameReplays.org listing page HTML parser + real HTML fixture" ``` **Report back:** - Status: DONE / BLOCKED - Test results (pass/fail count) - How many replay entries were parsed from the fixture - Any issues with the HTML structure

from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

    page.goto("https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php?game=2&show=index&tab_new=upcoming&sort_by=date_added&st=0", wait_until="networkidle", timeout=30000)

from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import re

with open("tests/data/fixtures/listing_page.html", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    html = f.read()

soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")

# Find li elements with download links
download_pat = re.compile(r"show=download")
li_with_download = []
for li in soup.find_all("li"):
    anchors = li.find_all("a", href=True)
    if any(download_pat.search(a.get("href", "")) for a in anchors):
        li_with_download.append(li)

print(f"Found {len(li_with_download)} <li> elements with download links")
if li_with_download:
    print("\nFirst matching <li>:")
    print(li_with_download[0].prettify()[:2000])
PYEOF

content.text: The actual HTML has CSS classes, not bare `<li>` — it uses `<div class="replay_title">` for the title, `<div class="matchup">` for player/faction info, etc. The parser needs to use these class selectors. Let me write the correct implementation.

source .venv/bin/activate && python - <<'PYEOF'
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import re

with open("tests/data/fixtures/listing_page.html", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    html = f.read()

soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")

# Check wubs structure
li_with_download = []
download_pat = re.compile(r"show=download")
for li in soup.find_all("li"):
    anchors = li.find_all("a", href=True)
    if any(download_pat.search(a.get("href", "")) for a in anchors):
        li_with_download.append(li)

li = li_with_download[0]
print("--- wubs div ---")
print(li.find("div", class_="replay_index_button index_wubs"))
print("--- title div ---")
print(li.find("div", class_="replay_title"))
print("--- information div ---")
print(li.find("div", class_="information"))
print("--- matchup div ---")
print(li.find("div", class_="matchup"))
print("--- download link ---")
dl = li.find("a", class_="replay_index_button index_download")
print(dl)
print("--- download href ---")
print(dl.get("href"))
print("--- id extraction ---")
m = re.search(r"[?&]id=(\d+)", dl.get("href", ""))
print(m.group(1) if m else "NOT FOUND")
PYEOF

content.text: Now I have a clear picture. The HTML uses CSS classes, not bare text nodes. Let me write the corrected implementation and tests.

input.content:
# tests/data/test_gamereplays.py
"""Tests for GameReplays.org HTML parsers."""

from pathlib import Path
import pytest
from zh_copilot.data.gamereplays import parse_listing_page, listing_page_url

FIXTURES = Path(__file__).parent / "fixtures"


@pytest.fixture
def listing_html():
    return (FIXTURES / "listing_page.html").read_text(encoding="utf-8")


def test_parse_listing_returns_list(listing_html):
    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
    assert isinstance(results, list)
    assert len(results) > 0


def test_parse_listing_entry_has_required_fields(listing_html):
    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
    entry = results[0]
    for field in ["id", "title", "uploader", "upload_date", "factions", "players", "download_count", "wubs", "game_version"]:
        assert field in entry, f"Missing field: {field}"


def test_parse_listing_id_is_int(listing_html):
    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
    assert isinstance(results[0]["id"], int)
    assert results[0]["id"] > 0


def test_parse_listing_download_count_is_int(listing_html):
    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
    assert isinstance(results[0]["download_count"], int)


def test_parse_listing_factions_is_list(listing_html):
    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
    assert isinstance(results[0]["factions"], list)


def test_parse_listing_players_is_list(listing_html):
    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
    assert isinstance(results[0]["players"], list)


def test_parse_listing_no_duplicate_ids(listing_html):
    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
    ids = [r["id"] for r in results]
    assert len(ids) == len(set(ids))


def test_parse_listing_returns_up_to_30(listing_html):
    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
    assert 1 <= len(results) <= 30


def test_listing_page_url_pagination():
    url0 = listing_page_url(offset=0)
    url1 = listing_page_url(offset=30)
    assert "st=0" in url0
    assert "st=30" in url1
    assert "game=2" in url0


def test_listing_page_url_unauthenticated():
    url = listing_page_url(offset=0, authenticated=False)
    assert "tab_new=upcoming" in url
    assert "search_submit" not in url


def test_listing_page_url_authenticated():
    url = listing_page_url(offset=0, authenticated=True)
    assert "search_submit" in url
    assert "tab_new" not in url

# src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py """HTML parsers for GameReplays.org — pure functions, no network calls. Actual HTML structure (confirmed April 2026): Each replay is a
  • with CSS-class child divs:
  • 0
    3 1
    ...
    Player1 Player2
    Title text
    By: uploader - Added DD Mon, YYYY - vX.XX
    • """ from __future__ import annotations import re from typing import Any from bs4 import BeautifulSoup _BASE = "https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php" _GAME = 2 # Zero Hour game ID _LISTING_URL_NOAUTH = ( f"{_BASE}?game={_GAME}&show=index&tab_new=upcoming&sort_by=date_added&st={{offset}}" ) _LISTING_URL_AUTH = ( f"{_BASE}?game={_GAME}&show=search_submit" f"&search=&matchup_type=all&army_1=all&army_2=all" f"&map=all&version=all&event=all&search_submit=clicked&st={{offset}}" ) def listing_page_url(offset: int = 0, authenticated: bool = False) -> str: """Return the listing page URL at the given offset. Args: offset: Pagination offset (increments of 30). authenticated: If True, uses the search endpoint (35,998 replays, requires session). If False (default), uses public endpoint (12,366 replays). """ template = _LISTING_URL_AUTH if authenticated else _LISTING_URL_NOAUTH return template.format(offset=offset) def detail_page_url(replay_id: int) -> str: """Return the detail page URL for a replay ID.""" return f"{_BASE}?s=0&game={_GAME}&show=details&id={replay_id}" def download_url(replay_id: int) -> str: """Return the download URL for a replay ID.""" return f"{_BASE}?s=0&game={_GAME}&show=download&&id={replay_id}" def parse_listing_page(html: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]: """Parse a listing page and return a list of replay metadata dicts. Parses the CSS-class-based
  • structure from the live site (confirmed April 2026). Returns list of dicts with: id, title, uploader, upload_date, factions, players, download_count, wubs, game_version. """ soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser") results: list[dict[str, Any]] = [] seen_ids: set[int] = set() id_pat = re.compile(r"[?&]id=(\d+)") for li in soup.find_all("li"): # Must have a download link (class contains index_download) download_a = li.find("a", class_="index_download") if download_a is None: continue # Extract replay ID from download link href href = download_a.get("href", "") m = id_pat.search(href) if not m: continue replay_id = int(m.group(1)) if replay_id in seen_ids: continue seen_ids.add(replay_id) # Wubs: text inside the index_wubs div wubs_div = li.find("div", class_="index_wubs") wubs = _parse_int(wubs_div.get_text(strip=True)) if wubs_div else 0 # Download count: text of the download link download_count = _parse_int(download_a.get_text(strip=True)) # Matchup div holds players and factions matchup_div = li.find("div", class_="matchup") factions: list[str] = [] players: list[str] = [] if matchup_div: for team_a in matchup_div.find_all("a", class_="matchup_teambg"): img = team_a.find("img") if img and img.get("alt", "").strip(): factions.append(img["alt"].strip()) # Player name: text inside the after stripping img text player_name = team_a.get_text(strip=True) if player_name: players.append(player_name) # Title: replay_title div title_div = li.find("div", class_="replay_title") title = title_div.get_text(strip=True) if title_div else "" # Information div: uploader, date, version info_div = li.find("div", class_="information") uploader = "" upload_date = "" game_version = "" if info_div: member_pat = re.compile(r"show=member_replays&(?:amp;)?member_id=") uploader_a = info_div.find("a", href=member_pat) uploader = uploader_a.get_text(strip=True) if uploader_a else "" info_text = info_div.get_text(" ", strip=True) upload_date = _extract_date(info_text) game_version = _extract_version(info_text) results.append({ "id": replay_id, "title": title, "uploader": uploader, "upload_date": upload_date, "factions": factions, "players": players, "download_count": download_count, "wubs": wubs, "game_version": game_version, }) return results def parse_detail_page(html: str, replay_id: int) -> dict[str, Any]: """Parse a replay detail page and return full metadata. Returns dict with: id, title, uploader, upload_date, factions, players, matchup_type, map_name, game_version, download_count, wubs, duration_seconds. """ soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser") full_text = soup.get_text(" ", strip=True) title_tag = soup.find("h1") or soup.find("h2") title = title_tag.get_text(strip=True) if title_tag else _extract_title(full_text, [], "") imgs = soup.find_all("img", alt=True) factions = [ img["alt"].strip() for img in imgs if img["alt"].strip() and len(img["alt"].strip()) < 30 and not img["alt"].strip().startswith(("/", "http")) ] player_pat = re.compile(r"search_player=") players = [a.get_text(strip=True) for a in soup.find_all("a", href=player_pat)] member_pat = re.compile(r"show=member_replays&(?:amp;)?member_id=") uploader_a = soup.find("a", href=member_pat) uploader = uploader_a.get_text(strip=True) if uploader_a else "" map_name = _extract_text_after(full_text, r"Map[:\s]+([^\n|]+?)(?:\s*[-|]|$)") matchup_type = _detect_matchup_type(full_text) duration_seconds = _extract_duration(full_text) upload_date = _extract_date(full_text) game_version = _extract_version(full_text) download_count = _parse_int(_extract_text_after(full_text, r"(\d[\d,]*)\s+[Dd]ownload")) wubs = _parse_int(_extract_text_after(full_text, r"(\d+)\s+[Ww]ub")) return { "id": replay_id, "title": title, "uploader": uploader, "upload_date": upload_date, "factions": factions[:2], "players": players[:2], "matchup_type": matchup_type, "map_name": (map_name or "").strip(), "game_version": game_version, "download_count": download_count, "wubs": wubs, "duration_seconds": duration_seconds, } # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Private helpers # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def _extract_title(full_text: str, players: list[str], uploader: str) -> str: text = full_text text = re.sub(r"\s*By:.*$", "", text, flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL).strip() for p in players: if p: text = text.replace(p, "").strip() text = re.sub(r"^\s*(\d+\s*)+", "", text).strip() return text def _extract_date(text: str) -> str: m = re.search(r"Added\s+(\d{1,2}\s+\w+,?\s+\d{4})", text, re.IGNORECASE) if m: return m.group(1) m = re.search(r"(\d{1,2}\s+\w{3,},?\s+\d{4})", text) return m.group(1) if m else "" def _extract_version(text: str) -> str: m = re.search(r"v(\d+\.\d+)", text) return m.group(1) if m else "" def _extract_text_after(text: str, pattern: str) -> str | None: m = re.search(pattern, text, re.IGNORECASE) if m: return m.group(1).strip() if m.lastindex else m.group(0).strip() return None def _detect_matchup_type(text: str) -> str: for matchup in ["4v4", "3v3", "2v2", "1v1", "FFA"]: if matchup.lower() in text.lower(): return matchup return "1v1" def _extract_duration(text: str) -> int | None: m = re.search(r"[Dd]uration[:\s]+(\d+):(\d+)", text) if m: return int(m.group(1)) * 60 + int(m.group(2)) m = re.search(r"(\d+)\s*min(?:ute)?s?\s+(\d+)\s*sec", text, re.IGNORECASE) if m: return int(m.group(1)) * 60 + int(m.group(2)) m = re.search(r"(\d+)\s*min(?:ute)?s?", text, re.IGNORECASE) if m: return int(m.group(1)) * 60 return None def _parse_int(s: str | None) -> int: if not s: return 0 m = re.search(r"\d[\d,]*", str(s)) return int(m.group(0).replace(",", "")) if m else 0

Status: DONE **Test results:** 11/11 passed (0.19s) **Entries parsed from fixture:** 30 (full page) **HTML structure correction:** The confirmed spec described bare `
  • ` with positional text nodes, but the live site uses CSS-class child divs. The actual structure is: - `
    ` — wubs count - `` — download count + ID extraction - `
    ` with `` children — each contains an `` and the player name as text - `
    ` — replay title - `
    ` — uploader link, date (`Added DD Mon, YYYY`), version (`vX.XX`) The implementation was adapted to use these class selectors instead of positional parsing. Sample first entry: id=369445, title="Usa mirror vs HARD", uploader="saptac", date="26 Mar, 2026", factions=["Vanilla USA", "Vanilla USA"], players=["saptac", "HARD^^"], download_count=3, wubs=0, version="1.04". **New files:** - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py` - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/tests/data/test_gamereplays.py` - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/tests/data/fixtures/listing_page.html`
